rules.title: Unicode bidirectional control character
rules.description: Source text or filename contains a Unicode BiDi control character.
rules.rationale: BiDi controls can make rendered code differ from compiler-visible code.
rules.remediation: Remove the control character and review the affected line in a hex-aware editor.
rules.attack_narrative: An attacker uses a right-to-left override to hide executable code behind a misleading comment.
attack_examples: A source line contains an RLO marker that changes display order.
false_positive_examples: Rare tests that intentionally validate Unicode sanitizer behavior.

rules.title: Cluster of zero-width characters
rules.description: Source text or filename contains repeated zero-width characters.
rules.rationale: Zero-width clusters can hide tokens from reviewers while preserving compiler-visible behavior.
rules.remediation: Remove the zero-width characters or replace the token with plain ASCII.
rules.attack_narrative: A variable name includes zero-width joiners to masquerade as a trusted identifier.
attack_examples: A token uses multiple zero-width joiners to evade visual review.

rules.title: Mixed-script identifier-like token
rules.description: An identifier-like token mixes Latin with Cyrillic or Greek confusable characters.
rules.rationale: Mixed-script identifiers can impersonate trusted names such as package or function identifiers.
rules.remediation: Rename the identifier using one script, preferably ASCII for security-sensitive code.
rules.attack_narrative: A variable named with a Cyrillic character visually matches a trusted Latin identifier.
attack_examples: The token nuсlear uses a Cyrillic small es in place of Latin c.

rules.title: Filename confusable collision
rules.description: Two entries in the same directory normalize to the same ASCII-like filename.
rules.rationale: Confusable filenames can hide a malicious file beside a trusted-looking one.
rules.remediation: Rename one of the files so normalized names are distinct and review imports/build references.
rules.attack_narrative: A package ships both config.js and соnfig.js, relying on visual similarity during review.
attack_examples: Two filenames differ only by Cyrillic confusables.
